page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores using an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The underlying hypervisor is KVM.

Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance but weaker multi-core scaling. The stress-ng single-core CPU test scores 1578.29 (average), while the multi-core score drops to a weak 4153.98. Similarly, Geekbench single-core performance is average at 1336.0, but the multi-core score of 2947.0 is weak. Memory bandwidth shows a similar pattern: large block write bandwidth is average at 25.51 GB/sec, but read bandwidth is weak at 30.99 GB/sec. Single-threaded decompression (bzip3) and text processing perform at average levels, whereas multi-threaded decompression is poor.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speed, the ecs.hfc7.xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require consistent single-threaded CPU performance without massive memory footprints. However, the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and weak multi-core scaling present a tradeoff for memory-intensive or highly parallelized tasks. This instance is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded batch processing, text processing, and small-scale application hosting where dedicated CPU execution is required but memory demands are low.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB per core, and includes no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmarks show average single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, but weak multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth. This hardware profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated single-threaded execution rather than high memory capacity. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded batch processing, and basic text processing tasks.

8.0 GB of system memory with no bundled local storage
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on KVM hypervisor
